signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Shock + Carry Unwind
signals.text: WTI Crude falls 9.46% to $92.6 and DXY drops below 98.00 as US-Iran peace MOU reports trigger an aggressive unwind of geopolitical risk premiums. USD/JPY at 156.255 reflects this shift; while the 300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75%) structurally supports the pair, the sudden removal of the energy supply-shock tailwind accelerates JPY short-covering. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 remains a crowded carry trade vulnerable to further liquidation if the Strait of Hormuz transit is confirmed safe. Carry dominance is suspended until energy prices stabilize.

signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Bias
signals.text: NZD/CAD exhibits a 1.160% G8 spread as NZD outperforms (+0.732% avg) while CAD lags (-0.428% avg). The divergence is driven by the RBNZ hike bias vs BoC hold stance, further amplified by the collapse in oil prices weighing on the Loonie. NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (23% long) vs RBNZ hike bias indicates a significant short-squeeze risk if the May 27 meeting confirms hawkishness. NZD/USD at 0.5969.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7248 shows a sharp divergence between institutional and retail positioning. COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) indicates heavy institutional backing, while retail sentiment is at an extreme 91% short. This 91% retail short position against a 60bp AUD carry premium (RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75%) suggests a high risk of retail capitulation if price holds above 0.7250. The energy link highlighted by OCBC provides additional fundamental support despite the broader oil sell-off.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Carry
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.735 vs 252d norm of -0.135 (z=+1.88σ) signals a regime shift where EUR is trading as a pro-cyclical risk asset rather than a funding currency. Despite the 175bp carry disadvantage (Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0%), EUR/USD at 1.1763 is buoyed by the 3.03% surge in STOXX 50 following the US-Iran peace headlines. Upside remains structurally capped by the yield differential unless US 10Y yields (currently 4.416%) break lower.
